staged_note: Reviewer-gated exogenous sources held in staged/ (see staged/README.md). NOT scanned by the top-level *.txt globs in bench_formalize.py, cgb.py _ledger_sync, or tests/test_mathsources_manifest.py's EXPECTED_TOTAL/bijection. Promotion (move file to top level, entry to `files`, bump EXPECTED_TOTAL, re-baseline bench) is a reviewer action. `file` values are relative to staged/. Fields beyond the `files` schema (existential, binder_hint, provenance, staged_reason) are staging-only metadata.

staged.staged_reason: primary WP-T6b witness: genuine bounded/finitizable exists (q, r); provides a non-forall binder the certified corpus currently lacks

provenance.verbatim: For every pair of integers a, b where b != 0, there exist unique integers q, r such that a = qb + r and 0 <= r < |b|
provenance.rendering_note: specialized to b > 0 (so |b| = b, staying inside the Nat/Int fragment without an abs operator); uniqueness dropped (fragment has no unique-exists)

staged.staged_reason: WP-T6b witness: bounded/finitizable exists (x, y) over gcd; exercises the gcd term operator under an existential

provenance.verbatim: there exist integers x and y such that ax + by = gcd(a, b)
provenance.rendering_note: gcd rendered as 'the greatest common divisor of a and b' (Int.gcd term operator)

staged.staged_reason: WP-T6b witness: clean forall-exists (for every n there is a greater m); finitizable shadow m = n + 1; mirrors the 28_predecessor forall-exists shape the T6b tooth re-authors

provenance.reference: The set of integers has no greatest element (standard result; e.g. Epp, Discrete Mathematics with Applications; ProofWiki Category:Greatest/Least Elements)
provenance.license: CC BY-SA (ProofWiki) / standard public educational text
provenance.verbatim: there is no greatest integer: for every integer there exists a strictly greater integer
provenance.rendering_note: the negation of 'the integers have a greatest element', stated in existential form

staged.staged_reason: WP-T6b witness: the existential unfolding of divisibility (a | b implies exists q, b = a*q); recurrence-central to the divides family

provenance.reference: Definition of divisibility (standard; e.g. LibreTexts, A Spiral Workbook for Discrete Mathematics)
provenance.verbatim: If a and b are integers, with a != 0, we say a divides b if there exists an integer q such that b = aq
provenance.rendering_note: stated as the forward implication (a | b implies the witness exists), avoiding the biconditional the fragment cannot form between a pred and an exists

staged.staged_reason: recurrence: congruent-mod family member (transitivity), parallels 33/34/35 and 11/12

provenance.reference: Congruence Modulo Integer is Equivalence Relation, ProofWiki
provenance.verbatim: if a === b (mod m) and b === c (mod m) then a === c (mod m)

staged.staged_reason: recurrence: congruent-mod family member (add a constant to both sides), parallels 33_cong_add

provenance.verbatim: if a === b (mod n) and c === d (mod n) then a + c === b + d (mod n)
provenance.rendering_note: specialized to c === c (add the same c to both sides)

staged.staged_reason: recurrence: congruent-mod family member (multiply both sides by a constant), parallels 34_cong_mul

provenance.verbatim: if a === b (mod n) and c === d (mod n) then ac === bd (mod n)
provenance.rendering_note: specialized to c === c (multiply both sides by the same c)

staged.staged_reason: recurrence: divides-both family member (g divides both implies g divides the sum), parallels 37_db_diff

provenance.reference: Common Divisor Divides Integer Combination, ProofWiki
provenance.verbatim: Let a, b be integers. Let d be a common divisor of a and b. Then d divides a + b (and any integer combination xa + yb)

staged.staged_reason: recurrence: common-divisor family member (linear combination with subtraction), parallels 32_cd_combo

provenance.reference: Common Divisor Divides Integer Combination, ProofWiki
provenance.verbatim: Let d be a common divisor of a and b. Then d divides any integer combination xa - yb of a and b

staged.staged_reason: recurrence: parity-algebra family member (product of evens), parallels 04/06/14

provenance.reference: Product of Even Integers is Even (standard parity result; ProofWiki Category:Even Integers)
provenance.verbatim: The product of two even integers is even

staged.staged_reason: deliberate boundary source: a genuinely existential statement (exists two primes) that EXCEEDS the fragment because primality (operator:prime) is absent; illustrates where the fragment's exists-reach ends. Marked as 38_infinitude_primes is (non-transcribable, miss operator:prime)

provenance.verbatim: every even integer greater than 2 can be expressed as the sum of two prime numbers
